content.claim: Early tracking via Hungary's 6- and 8-year gimnázium programs, operating at ages 10-11 and 12-13, is independently linked to major educational inequality beyond prior ability differences, with secondary school tracks highly stratified by family socioeconomic status and affecting later educational and career opportunities.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Equity and social mobility lens
limitations: This is one research lineage rather than an uncontested consensus on causal attribution
The tracking effect's causal separation from prior SES differences relies on single authors' models
No direct experimental randomization isolates the causal effect of the 6/8-year mechanism from the broader 3-track secondary system
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
